title: Community Shelters * Homeless Women - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Community Shelters * Homeless Women 18 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Ex-Offenders Homeless Couples Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Homeless Youth Mothers Pregnant Individuals BLANCHET HOUSE OF HOSPITALITY BETHANIE'S ROOM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier, overnight shelter. Guests will have access to peer support specialists and the ability to store belongings at the site during the day. 1015 NW 17th Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female, transgender, or non-binary. Pets allowed. Hours: Intake: 7 days per week 7pm-8pm. Returning guests are guaranteed their bed provided they arrive by 8pm. Shelter: 7 days per week 8pm-8am Email (971) 358-7744 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES CITY LIGHT HOME FOR WOMEN AND CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers meals and emergency shelter. Shelter services include clothing provision, education, work-search assistance, mental health counseling, addiction recovery through the New Life Program, children’s programs, and coordination services with Boise Veteran's Affairs for homeless female veterans. Eligible community members will not be turned away for food or a clean bed due to lack of space. 1404 W Jefferson Street Boise, ID 83702 Eligibility: Women experiencing homelessness, women with children, and women who are veterans Hours: Shelter check in: 7 days per week 4pm Breakfast: Sunday-Friday 6am, Saturday 8am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Email (208) 368-9901 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES VALLEY WOMEN AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers overnight emergency shelter. Services at the shelter include meals, clothing, and case management. Boise Rescue Mission Ministries has a policy to never turn down anyone for food or shelter due to a lack of space. 869 W Corporate Lane Nampa, ID 83651 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who are experiencing homelessness or have minor children Hours: Overnight shelter check-in: 7 days per week 4pm Email (208) 475-0725 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION FIKSO FAMILY CENTER WOMEN'S SHELTER WOMEN AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter for women and children, subject to review every 30 days. Residents must attend morning and evening chapel and Sunday church services. 530 SW Foundry Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Single women and women with children age 17 and younger. No smoking or drug use allowed. No male visitors are allowed in the shelter at any time. Hours: Intake Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Shelter hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 200-6242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH FALLS GOSPEL MISSION 30-DAY DRUG-FREE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates an emergency/short term shelter that provides daily meals, showers, and clothing. Clients must be able to care for themselves and willing to do a 4 hour chore. 1931 Mission Avenue Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Age 18 and older. Prospective guests must be 72 hours free of substance use before intake. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3:30pm Email (541) 882-4895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter locator with bed availability in Lane County. Not all shelters in Lane County are listed. 151 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by shelter Hours: Not applicable (541) 682-3798 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter. Guests will be served a hot meal and have access to showers, laundry, toilietries, a clothing closet. phone and internet access, socks, and underwear. Maximum capacity is 22 individuals and beds are available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 1040 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older, emancipated youth, and families with minor children Hours: Intake: 7 days per week 6pm-6am Check-in: 7 days per week 6pm-8pm Email (503) 468-8176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE CHESTNUT TREE INN MOTEL MOTEL SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides waitlist based motel shelter for individuals experiencing homelessness. Eligibility: Current residents of Gresham Women's Shelter screened via a risk assessment Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week for current participants. 11:30pm curfew, but accommodations can be made if job-related. Email (503) 255-4444 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE GRESHAM WOMEN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a low-barrier, reservation-based shelter for 90 women, with bunk beds. If unable to access a top bunk, reasonable accommodations may be made if available. Guests will be able to store a small amount of belongings in a secure 3ft locker and use the kitchen, on-site laundry, and showers. Community food storage limited to one cloth grocery bag for cupboard and one gallon size zip bag for fridge per person. Non-perishable food items allowed in personal locker. During the day, guests can use the computer center and access services through referrals with Human Solutions and community partners. Services include assistance finding housing, employment, veterans' services, and mental health and addictions services. There is no time limit on stay at the shelter; guests can stay as long as needed. Eligibility: Female-identified adults age 18 and older. Pregnant women in any trimester of pregnancy. No minors under 18 years old. Top Bunk: Must be able to access a top bunk Bottom Bunk: Bottom bunks are reserved for individuals who are physically unable to access a top bunk. Shelter MAY require documentation from a medical professional stating that a medical restriction prevents access to a top bunk within one week of accessing shelter. One cat or dog is allowed. No other or additional pets are accepted. Pets must have a kennel. Pet can be outside of their kennels while inside the building, as long the owner has control of the animal and it is on a leash. Guests have up to 72 hours to get their own kennel. Hours: Waitlist: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Business hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week for current residents. Curfew is at 11pm. Late entry that is job-related can be arranged during intake. Closed Monday-Friday 12:30pm-3pm for cleaning. Email (971) 333-1223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REDEMPTION HOUSE MINISTRIES REDEMPTION HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional shelter with a maximum capacity of 17 individuals. A shower and load of laundry is offered for guests. Dinner and sack breakfast provided for people staying overnight. 780 E 1st Street Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who are experiencing homelessness or have minor children Hours: Monday-Friday 4pm-8am Email (541) 362-5642 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG RESCUE MISSION SAMARITAN INN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter with 110 beds including meals, showers, laundry facilities, and clothing. Children must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian. 544 W Umpqua Street Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Women over age 18, pregnant women, and women with children (boys up to age 14). No pets accepted. Hours: Check in: 6pm for new residents; earlier arrangements can be made if referred by a social service organization, law enforcement, or medical provider Shelter curfew: 6:30pm Meals: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 7am-7:30am, Saturday-Sunday 8am-8:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday noon-12:30pm, Saturday-Sunday 1pm-1:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 5pm-5:30pm (541) 672-5387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY PORTLAND METRO BURNSIDE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an overnight shelter with 100 beds. Provides food, sleeping mats, and hygiene supplies. 30 SW 2nd Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older who identify as women. Pets permitted as space allows. Hours: 7 days per week 8pm-8am Email (503) 227-0810 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY PORTLAND METRO MOORE STREET SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an overnight shelter with 100 beds. Provides food, sleeping mats, and hygiene supplies. 5325 N Williams Avenue Portland, OR 97217 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who identify as female. Pets permitted as space allows. Hours: 7 days per week 8pm-6am Email (971) 340-4010 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS LAURELWOOD CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shelter and service center for up to 120 people experiencing homelessness. The site has a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day and provides onsite meals. Staff will provide housing-focused support, employment and benefits assistance, and health-focused services. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 6130 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older or couples (any two people who present as a couple, regardless of gender) who are experiencing homelessness. Priority is given to people age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS WILLAMETTE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shelter and service center for up to 120 people experiencing homelessness. The site has 2 large sleeping rooms, a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day, and a kitchen for light food and beverages. Staff will provide information, referral, and support. Onsite support services include housing, health, and income resources. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 5120 SE Milwaukie Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older or couples (any two people who present as a couple, regardless of gender). Priority is given to people age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM SIMONKA PLACE FOR WOMEN AND CHILDREN SIMONKA PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter. Services available to guests include food, emergency clothing, laundry, mail services, faith-based addiction and mental health counseling, and case management. Guests may have the opportunity to move forward into the New Life Fellowship transitional housing program. Shelter availability may vary depending on the time of day. 5119 River Road N Keizer, OR 973030431 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who are experiencing homelessness or have minor children (boys must be 17 years or younger). Service animals accepted. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 362-7487 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED WAY MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY SAFE SLEEP UNITED Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a low barrier, overnight shelter for women. Personal items are limited to what can be stored under half of the under-bed space. The location is scent free. No outside food or drinks are permitted. No drugs, alcohol, paraphernalia, or weapons are permitted. Dinner and continental breakfast provided. 1910 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Women experiencing homelessness. Pets are accepted as space allows. They must be well behaved and under their owner's supervision at all times. Guests must also be able to do the following: -Get around, eat, clean up, and use the restroom independently -Use the restroom quickly -Shower elsewhere at least once a week (no onsite shower) -Make their bed each morning and change the bedding once a week -Do one chore each day -Be willing and able to be up and out of the shelter by 7:15am each morning Hours: 7 days per week 5:45pm-7:15am Dinner: 6:30pm Continental breakfast: 5:30am-6:30am Email (503) 816-3409 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION WOMEN'S FACILITY 30 DAY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 30-day short-term shelter. Services include a clothing closet, showers, and hygiene items. Shelter space is limited. Unaccompanied minors may be eligible for services with the permission of a guardian or law enforcement agency. 1340 NE Logan Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with children. Guests must abstain from substance use for the duration of their stay. Animals are allowed. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Doors lock at 10pm. Email (503) 472-9766 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLANCHET HOUSE OF HOSPITALITY BETHANIE'S ROOM GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION WOMEN AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER KLAMATH FALLS GOSPEL MISSION 30-DAY DRUG-FREE SHELTER LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER LIFEBOAT SERVICES LIFEBOAT SERVICES REDEMPTION HOUSE MINISTRIES REDEMPTION HOUSE ROSEBURG RESCUE MISSION SAMARITAN INN SALVATION ARMY PORTLAND METRO BURNSIDE SHELTER SALVATION ARMY PORTLAND METRO MOORE STREET SHELTER TRANSITION PROJECTS LAURELWOOD CENTER TRANSITION PROJECTS WILLAMETTE CENTER UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM SIMONKA PLACE UNITED WAY MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY SAFE SLEEP UNITED YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION 30 DAY SHELTER
